title: Compositions To Add — Pattern Usage Coverage Worklist
description: Tracks catalog patterns that lack a real, documented usage in a shipping framework, application, or service, and the candidate compositions found to fill each gap. A pattern with zero documented uses is unproven in practice. Items graduate by having a verified usage added to a compositions-src/ shard. Generated and refreshed by _build_compositions_todo.py; candidate research is hand-added and preserved across regenerations. Pure data, no code.

items.description: One entry per pattern lacking documented framework/app/service usage.

usage_count.description: Current count of framework compositions implementing this pattern (non-anti-pattern role).

avoided_count.description: Anti-patterns only: count of frameworks that explicitly design against this anti-pattern.

status.description: todo = not yet researched; scanning = research in progress; candidate-found = ≥1 evidenced candidate recorded, not yet wired into a shard; added = usage written to compositions-src/ (item will drop on next regen); no-public-usage = research done, no documented real-world usage found (pattern stays experimental).

candidates.description: Real frameworks/apps/services found to implement this pattern, with upstream evidence.

composition_id.description: Existing composition id to extend, or proposed id for a new composition.

note.description: How the framework realises the pattern (class, decorator, config, workflow).

verified.description: True once the evidence url+quote has been confirmed against upstream.

composition_candidates.description: New framework/app/service compositions worth authoring (e.g. a successor product that an existing entry points to but that is not yet catalogued). Each becomes a full kind=framework entry in a compositions-src/ shard once researched, adding usage refs to the patterns it implements.

reason.description: Why it belongs in the catalog (e.g. successor of <id>, referenced by <id>).

predecessor.description: If this is a successor, the existing composition id it replaces (re-add its successor field once authored).

covers.description: Zero-usage pattern ids this product implements (the reason to author it). A composition needs >=2 members; products covering only one listed pattern need a second well-known member found during authoring.

needs_second_member.description: True when 'covers' has a single pattern — authoring requires finding one more pattern this product documents to satisfy the >=2 members rule.

authoring_candidates.description: New patterns (often anti-patterns) worth authoring because a real framework already documents the behaviour — authoring them yields a pattern that ships with a documented usage. Distinct from pattern-todo.json (general candidates); these are specifically surfaced by composition reference work.
